title: astartea arbuscula
intro: Astartea arbuscula, also known as the paperbark tea tree, is a small shrub native to Australia, characterized by its distinctive white or cream-colored bark that peels off in papery layers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, with medicinal properties attributed to its essential oils, particularly eucalyptol and limonene, which exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Astartea arbuscula include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Astartea arbuscula can be prepared as a tea by infusing the leaves and bark in hot water, or as a tincture by extracting the essential oils with a solvent.
